stem: In convex pentagon PQRST, segment PQ is parallel to segment ST. The measure of angle Q is 120 degrees, and the measure of angle S is 156 degrees. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle R?
rationale: The sum of the interior angles of a convex pentagon is 540°. Since PQ ∥ ST, the angles at P and T (formed with the adjacent sides PE and SR meeting transversally) sum to 180° (co-interior angles), so ∠P + ∠T = 180°. Therefore ∠Q + ∠R + ∠S = 540 − 180 = 360°, giving ∠R = 360 − 120 − 156 = 84°.
